title: The Alma Matter

jokes.text: Don't worry, Will, I made breakfast. / Damn, I'm full. Couldn't eat another bite.
jokes.setup: Hilary offers to feed the hungry Will with her cooking
jokes.punchline: Will immediately claims to be full just from the offer
jokes.explanation: Strong character-based humor establishing Hilary's terrible cooking. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: Come on, Will. I know what I'm doing. Remember, I was a professional caterer.
jokes.setup: Hilary defends her cooking credentials by citing professional experience
jokes.punchline: The irony of claiming expertise while being notoriously bad
jokes.explanation: Setup for the punchline to follow. Builds character consistency.

jokes.text: And you still would be if you hadn't poisoned those nuns.
jokes.setup: Hilary's claim about being a professional caterer
jokes.punchline: Revelation that she literally poisoned nuns
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation to absurd dark humor. Perfect economy and unexpectedly specific target (nuns).

jokes.text: The autopsy was inconclusive.
jokes.setup: Will's accusation that she poisoned nuns
jokes.punchline: Hilary's casual, legalistic defense implying people actually died
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan escalation. The casual mention of autopsy suggests actual deaths while maintaining plausible deniability.

jokes.text: These eggs aren't bad. / What's the crunchy stuff? / Great, you found my contact.
jokes.setup: Will tries to be polite about Hilary's cooking and asks about texture
jokes.punchline: The 'crunchy stuff' is Hilary's lost contact lens
jokes.explanation: Solid gross-out humor that pays off the bad cooking setup. Effective cringe moment.

jokes.text: And the guy getting slapped by the girl? / That's Clarence Thomas.
jokes.setup: Continuing the fraternity photo descriptions
jokes.punchline: Reference to Supreme Court Justice with sexual harassment history
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to Clarence Thomas hearings. Smart cultural commentary for the era.

jokes.text: My dining club's what made me the man I am today. / No, that's too easy.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil sets up an easy joke about his weight/character
jokes.punchline: Will acknowledges the setup but refuses to take the obvious shot
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor where the character acknowledges the obvious joke. Shows Will's respect for Phil.

jokes.text: If you don't know which seat is his... it'll be the one with the legs all bent out up from under it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Uncle Phil's club seat
jokes.punchline: Will takes the free fat joke that he passed up earlier
jokes.explanation: Callback to the 'too easy' moment. Will couldn't resist after all.

jokes.text: Come on, I gave you a free one earlier.
jokes.setup: Will's previous restraint
jokes.punchline: Explicit acknowledgment that he's now cashing in the setup
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the joke structure itself. Shows writing sophistication.

jokes.text: You gotta be facing somebody's butt to do this handshake?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil making Will turn around for secret handshake
jokes.punchline: Will's logical but crude interpretation of the positioning
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about absurd fraternity rituals.

jokes.text: If they was looking for a few good Pygmies, you'd have been a shoe in.
jokes.setup: Carlton's confidence about Princeton wanting 'a few good men'
jokes.punchline: Will's height joke using military recruitment slogan format
jokes.explanation: Classic Will vs Carlton height dynamic. Good wordplay on military slogan.

jokes.text: there's no one at Bel-Air Prep that comes close to having my grades... or my willingness to flatter authority figures... without a thought to personal dignity.
jokes.setup: Carlton listing his qualifications for Princeton
jokes.punchline: He includes shameless brown-nosing as an asset
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - accidentally admitting to having no dignity while being proud of it.

jokes.text: By the way, you look really great in denim.
jokes.setup: Carlton just described his willingness to flatter authority
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates by complimenting Uncle Phil
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate demonstration of the character trait he just described. Excellent callback structure.

jokes.text: Man, I tell you, you ain't got to worry about me applying... to no old Bugie or Muffy and Biffo... chicken-head old kind of...
jokes.setup: Will explaining why he won't apply to Princeton
jokes.punchline: His colorful description of preppy Princeton stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Great character voice with creative preppy name inventions. Shows Will's class consciousness.

jokes.text: Will, the standards of the Jim-Bob School of Trucking are pretty darn high.
jokes.setup: Will claiming he's decided where he wants to go to college
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's sarcastic assumption it's a lowbrow trade school
jokes.explanation: Good class-based humor showing Phil's assumptions about Will's ambitions.

jokes.text: Simple, I'm here to pick up the shattered pieces of your self-esteem... to nourish you in your hour of defeat.
jokes.setup: Will asks why Carlton is at the Princeton interview early
jokes.punchline: Carlton's elaborately fake concern for Will
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton pomposity - overly elaborate language for simple schadenfreude.

jokes.text: Because when I come out of here a broken man... you wanna be the first person to rub my nose in it? / No, I'm really here for you, Will. / Then why did you bring a video camera?
jokes.setup: Carlton's fake altruism exposed by Will
jokes.punchline: Physical evidence (video camera) contradicts Carlton's claims
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline with physical evidence undermining Carlton's lie.

jokes.text: Mr. Will Smith. / Yo. / 'Yo'?
jokes.setup: Formal Princeton interview begins with proper address
jokes.punchline: Will responds with street slang, interviewer's shocked reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect culture clash moment - Will's casual response vs. formal setting creates immediate tension and humor

jokes.text: Well, actually, I wanna go to Sea World, but is Princeton along the way?
jokes.setup: Interviewer asks if Will wants to go to Princeton
jokes.punchline: Will's completely unexpected priority (Sea World) with logical follow-up
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect character logic. Shows Will's irreverent attitude toward prestige.

jokes.text: You're funny. / We frown on that at Princeton.
jokes.setup: Ed acknowledges Will's humor
jokes.punchline: Immediate dismissal of humor as unwelcome at Princeton
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and satire of stuffy academic institutions. Sets up recurring 'frown' motif.

jokes.text: Unless one counts detention, in which case you lead the league.
jokes.setup: Previous statement about no extracurricular activities
jokes.punchline: Detention as mock extracurricular achievement, continuing baseball metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the baseball metaphor with detention as achievement.

jokes.text: But my jacket is a fashion miracle, though, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Ed's devastating academic critique
jokes.punchline: Will focuses on completely irrelevant fashion compliment-seeking
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Will's confidence and priority on style over academics.

jokes.text: And your hat is on backwards. / We frown on that at Princeton. / Y'all frown on a lot of stuff at Princeton.
jokes.setup: Previous 'frown' comment about humor
jokes.punchline: Will's observation that Princeton disapproves of everything
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds the pattern of Princeton's uptight nature.

jokes.text: Princeton has some of the most intelligent young women in the country. / There's no way I would make a crass generalization about such a diverse group. / Any cute honeys? / No.
jokes.setup: Ed's politically correct description of Princeton women
jokes.punchline: Immediate blunt 'No' when Will asks the direct question
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of PC language with brutal honesty. Great timing and structure.

jokes.text: Will solves the Rubik's cube
jokes.setup: Ed has been struggling with cube for months, Will takes it casually
jokes.punchline: Will immediately solves what stumped the Princeton interviewer
jokes.explanation: Good visual reversal showing Will's hidden intelligence despite poor academics.

jokes.text: Peace out, E-Money.
jokes.setup: Formal interview concluding
jokes.punchline: Will's casual, street-style goodbye to the uptight interviewer
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Will's casual attitude toward authority.

jokes.text: I don't know, the size of Barry White's hot tub.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks about acreage in a square mile
jokes.punchline: Will's completely irrelevant but vivid comparison
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and specific image. Great cultural reference that fits Will's character.

jokes.text: I'm guessing that one wasn't Madonna.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks about serious political quote from Zapata
jokes.punchline: Will's obviously wrong but contextually funny guess
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between serious political quote and pop culture reference.

jokes.text: I told him about the hood back in Philly. And he told me about some stuff called Spam.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his connection with the Princeton interviewer
jokes.punchline: The cultural exchange between urban and privileged experiences
jokes.explanation: Great class commentary showing the cultural divide. The idea that Ed doesn't know Spam is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Did my lips say Volvo? My lips meant to say Jag.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil agrees to let Will use the Volvo
jokes.punchline: Will immediately pushes for a more expensive car
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's opportunistic nature and newfound leverage.

jokes.text: Yo, what's up?
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to imitate Will's casual style for his interview
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at street language
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Carlton completely out of his element.

jokes.text: You don't have to call me Mr. Banks. My posse calls me Rap Master C.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to sound cool
jokes.punchline: His hilariously inappropriate street persona attempt
jokes.explanation: Iconic Carlton cringe moment. 'Rap Master C' became a quotable character moment.

jokes.text: Rap Master is my name. Ask me again and I'll tell you the same.
jokes.setup: Carlton's rap persona
jokes.punchline: His attempt at rap rhyming is painfully bad
jokes.explanation: The badness of the rap is intentionally funny. Shows Carlton's complete lack of street credibility.

jokes.text: Didn't mean to dis you, can I get you a tissue?
jokes.setup: Carlton continuing his rap attempt
jokes.punchline: Another terrible forced rhyme
jokes.explanation: Continues the cringe rap sequence. The awkwardness is the joke.

jokes.text: Let me teach you how to vogue. Strike a pose.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to be hip and current
jokes.punchline: His attempt at voguing in a Princeton interview
jokes.explanation: Extends Carlton's inappropriate interview behavior. Cultural reference to voguing trend.

jokes.text: Mr. Banks, are you on some sort of medication?
jokes.setup: Carlton's completely bizarre interview behavior
jokes.punchline: Ed's reasonable but understated reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction to Carlton's absurdity. Understated and logical.

jokes.text: Unfortunately, at this time... Princeton doesn't accept the clinically insane.
jokes.setup: Ed's assessment of Carlton's behavior
jokes.punchline: Clinical diagnosis as reason for rejection
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from 'medication' to 'clinically insane.' Sharp and quotable.

jokes.text: See where Princeton comes in? Right after 'lose virginity.'
jokes.setup: Carlton showing his life plan to the interviewer
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate personal information in formal interview setting
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy. The casualness of sharing intimate details in interview context is brilliant.

jokes.text: You know, my sister Hilary's not bad-looking and after two drinks or so... she'll do just about anything
jokes.setup: Carlton desperately trying to bribe the interviewer
jokes.punchline: Offering his sister in increasingly inappropriate terms
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Carlton's desperation. Shows how far he'll go for Princeton.

jokes.text: Big friends with lots of body hair and mozzarella on their breath. Capisce?
jokes.setup: Carlton threatening the interviewer
jokes.punchline: His hilariously specific description of imaginary mob connections
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity in the threat. 'Mozzarella on their breath' is unexpectedly vivid and funny.

jokes.text: Because Carlton doesn't have any friends... and I'll have to dance with him.
jokes.setup: Discussion of inviting Carlton's friends to celebration party
jokes.punchline: Will's observation about Carlton's social isolation and its consequences
jokes.explanation: Good character observation with logical progression to personal consequence.

jokes.text: you big poopy.
jokes.setup: Carlton angry at Will for ruining his life
jokes.punchline: His pathetically weak insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - even his anger is childish and ineffective.

jokes.text: 'Cause I don't say stuff like 'big poopy'?
jokes.setup: Carlton asking why Will gets everything he wants
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate callback to Carlton's weak insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent immediate callback with perfect economy. Shows Will's quick wit.

jokes.text: No offense, Tom, but I always thought my guardian angel would be black.
jokes.setup: Tom Jones as guardian angel
jokes.punchline: Carlton's practical observation about angel demographics
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about racial expectations in fantasy scenarios.

jokes.text: Well, I knew Otis Redding.
jokes.setup: Carlton's concern about angel's race
jokes.punchline: Tom's weak credential of knowing a black musician
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the setup. Shows celebrity name-dropping as weak qualification.

jokes.text: But I could probably pull some strings and have you moved opposite Roseanne.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking to have never been born
jokes.punchline: TV industry joke about being scheduled opposite a hit show
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about TV scheduling. Shows awareness of show's competitive landscape.

jokes.text: 'Good morning'? You sure you don't mean, 'Die, you gravy-sucking pig'?
jokes.setup: Carlton's polite morning greeting
jokes.punchline: Will's reference to Carlton's previous hostility with absurd specificity
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalation. 'Gravy-sucking pig' is more creative than Carlton's actual insults.

jokes.text: Go easy on him, Uncle Phil. He's a moron.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil wants to talk to Carlton privately
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt assessment delivered as helpful advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice. Will's directness contrasts with family diplomacy.

jokes.text: I could never like Will. Well, I mean, I like him... Wait a minute, here I am lying to you.
jokes.setup: Phil trying to reassure Carlton about favoritism
jokes.punchline: His honest admission that he was about to lie
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Phil's honesty overriding his diplomacy. Good self-awareness.

jokes.text: There's no need to be cruel.
jokes.setup: Phil saying Carlton can only be himself
jokes.punchline: Carlton's interpretation of this as an insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton self-deprecation. His low self-esteem makes sincere advice sound like an attack.

tmdb_overview: Depressed and suicidal over having ruined his chances of attending Princeton University, Carlton is visited by his Guardian Angel, who shows him how awful the Banks family would be if he weren't alive.
